ground_truth.description: On top of Eagles Nest in Berchtesgaden, Hitler's old cabin. Mountain appearing above clouds. Don't expect cabin recognition (only one wall barely visible), should understand high vantage point and Alps.
ground_truth.photographer_description: On top of the Eagles Nest in Berchtesgaden, Hitler's old cabin. You can see a mountain appearing above the clouds. I don't expect it to recognize the cabin, as only one wall is barely visible on the side of the photo, but it should at least understand that the photo is taken from a vantage point high up and hopefully recognize that it is the Alps. Daytime, over the clouds.
ground_truth.notes: Daytime, above clouds. Cabin not clearly visible.

details.description: Close-up of the corner of a high‑alpine stone building with rough‑cut granite block masonry, dark-stained timber eaves, and a metal rain gutter/downspout carrying a small icicle. Beyond the building, an expansive view opens over a sea of clouds to snow‑covered Alpine peaks under a deep blue sky. The combination of heavy granite construction and mountaintop panorama strongly suggests the Kehlsteinhaus (Eagle’s Nest) on Kehlstein above Berchtesgaden, with the Watzmann massif likely visible in the distance.

details.description: An alpine scene focusing on the corner of a mountain hut or stone residence. Foreground shows a textured stone-brick wall with a dark wooden eave and a metal downspout, lightly dusted with snow along the roof edge. The structure sits high above a sea of clouds, with a jagged, snow‑covered mountain ridge visible on the horizon under a clear bright blue sky. The lighting is crisp and high-contrast, typical of a cold, sunny high-altitude day in the Bavarian Alps.

details.description: Close-up mountain view from a high-altitude alpine lodge. In the foreground is the corner of a rough-cut stone wall with dark timber eaves and a metal rain gutter/downspout, edged with frost and a small icicle. Beyond, a sea of clouds sits below snow-covered peaks of the Berchtesgaden Alps under a clear blue winter sky. The perspective suggests standing at or next to the Kehlsteinhaus (Eagle’s Nest) on the Kehlstein above Berchtesgaden, looking out over the surrounding mountains.

details.description: An alpine scene photographed from a stone building. In the foreground, a snow-dusted wooden eave and a gray downspout run along a rough stone wall. Beyond, a rugged, snow-covered mountain peak rises through a low cloud layer, beneath a clear blue sky. The composition contrasts the man-made masonry and roofline with the expansive, wintry Alps and drifting clouds.

details.description: A vertical, low-angle photograph capturing the corner of the historic Kehlsteinhaus (Eagle's Nest) building against a backdrop of high-altitude alpine scenery. The foreground features the structure's distinctive rough-hewn granite masonry wall and a dark, weathered timber roof overhang. A metal rain gutter and downspout run along the eaves, adorned with small hanging icicles that indicate freezing temperatures. To the left, the view opens to a vibrant blue sky with wispy cirrus clouds. Below, a dense 'sea of clouds' (inversion layer) obscures the valley floor, while a rugged, snow-dusted peak of the Berchtesgaden Alps rises prominently in the distance, piercing the cloud cover.

details.description: A close-up shot of the corner of a stone building, likely the Kehlsteinhaus, featuring rough-hewn granite blocks and a dark wooden roof overhang. A metal gutter runs along the roofline, showing signs of frost or light snow. In the background, a dramatic alpine landscape unfolds with a thick sea of clouds partially obscuring the lower mountains, while a prominent, snow-covered peak, likely the Watzmann, rises into a clear, deep blue sky.

details.description: Close-up view from beneath the eaves of a stone-and-timber mountain chalet. The foreground shows a dark-stained wooden roof edge with a galvanized metal rain gutter and elbowed downspout, edged with frost/ice. The wall is built of light-colored squared stone blocks (ashlar). Beyond the building, a dramatic winter mountain scene opens: snow-dusted rocky peaks rise above a cloud deck, under a deep blue sky with thin streaks of cirrus clouds. Lighting is crisp and high-altitude, suggesting cold conditions at or near the snowline.

details.description: Rotated image showing the corner of a stone masonry building with ashlar-style blocks. A gray metal rain gutter curves along the roof edge and connects to a downspout that extends downward. A dark wooden beam runs along the eave, with a thin layer of snow clinging to its edge. In the background to the right, a snow-covered alpine peak rises through partly cloudy skies, with a band of clouds wrapping the mountain. The scene suggests a high-altitude chalet or mountain refuge with traditional stone-and-wood construction against a dramatic Alpine landscape.

details.description: Oblique winter mountain scene shot from beneath the eaves of a stone-and-timber chalet. In the foreground, a dark wooden roof edge with a metal gutter and downspout shows rime and small icicles. The wall is light stone masonry. Beyond the roofline, a vivid blue sky with thin streaks of high clouds opens over a dramatic, snow-dusted mountain ridge. Low clouds or fog spill through the valleys, partially shrouding the peaks, suggesting cold, windy conditions at high elevation.

details.description: The image shows the corner of a mountain building with a rough brick-and-stone wall. A white metal downspout with an elbow runs from the wall, connected to a gutter that runs along a dark wooden beam supported by the building. A light dusting of snow sits on the beam and along the edges where snow accumulates. In the distance, a dramatic snow-covered mountain range rises through low-lying clouds, set against a clear blue sky with wispy high-altitude clouds. The photo appears to be taken from a terrace or exterior vantage point at altitude, focusing on the juxtaposition of architectural detail in the foreground and dramatic alpine scenery in the background.

details.description: A low-angle, close-up architectural detail of a stone building corner set against a dramatic alpine backdrop. The image appears to be rotated 90 degrees; in proper orientation, a heavy wall constructed of rough-hewn, rectangular granite blocks stands on the right, topped by dark, weathered wooden eaves with visible frost or light snow. A metal drainpipe curves from the roofline. To the left, a deep blue sky is visible, framing a distant, jagged mountain peak rising sharply out of a dense layer of clouds or mist. The architectural style is characteristic of sturdy alpine construction, specifically matching the masonry of the Kehlsteinhaus.

details.description: A vertically oriented photograph taken from a low angle, looking up past the architectural details of a building towards a mountain landscape. In the foreground, a wall of light-colored, textured stone blocks meets dark wooden roof eaves. A metal gutter with a downspout is attached to the building, showing traces of frost or light snow. The background features a deep blue sky with wispy cirrus clouds and the prominent, snow-covered jagged peak of a mountain rising above a bank of clouds.
